title: feat(asset): introduce immutable asset version and project manifest
body:
## Summary

Implement the Cloud Asset Foundation for KusShoes.

### Features

- Immutable Asset Version
- Asset Version Files
- Legacy Compatibility Layer
- Project Asset Manifest
- Asset Download Endpoint
- ORM Refactor
- Alembic Migration
- Comprehensive Tests

### Validation

- 68/68 tests passed
- Alembic upgrade/downgrade verified
- Manual API verification completed
- Backward compatibility preserved

title: UI: Remove Reconstruction Readiness Banner & System Metrics Panel
body:
## Summary
This Pull Request removes the Reconstruction Readiness banner (`<ReadinessBanner>`) and its associated metrics (RAM, Storage, Threads) from the non-desktop frontend editor UI, as requested.

## Key Changes
- **Component Removal**: Deleted `ReadinessBanner` and `ReadinessBannerProps` in `frontend/src/App.tsx`.
- **State & Logic Cleanup**: 
  - Removed `readiness` state.
  - Removed `loadReadiness()` function and its API payload fetch logic.
  - Cleared the related `useEffect` call.
  - Removed `ReconstructionReadiness` type import.
- **UI Simplification**: Adjusted the rendering logic in `App.tsx` to conditionally render `<DesktopRuntimePanel>` only when `isDesktopShell` is active.

## Verification
- Verified code compilation locally using `npm run build` in the `frontend` workspace.
- The build succeeded with no TypeScript errors or bundler issues.

title: UI: Remove Reconstruction Readiness Banner & System Metrics Panel
body:
## Summary
This Pull Request removes the Reconstruction Readiness banner (`<ReadinessBanner>`) and its associated metrics (RAM, Storage, Threads) from the non-desktop frontend editor UI, as requested.

## Key Changes
- **Component Removal**: Deleted `ReadinessBanner` and `ReadinessBannerProps` in `frontend/src/App.tsx`.
- **State & Logic Cleanup**: 
  - Removed `readiness` state.
  - Removed `loadReadiness()` function and its API payload fetch logic.
  - Cleared the related `useEffect` call.
  - Removed `ReconstructionReadiness` type import.
- **UI Simplification**: Adjusted the rendering logic in `App.tsx` to conditionally render `<DesktopRuntimePanel>` only when `isDesktopShell` is active.

## Verification
- Verified code compilation locally using `npm run build` in the `frontend` workspace.
- The build succeeded with no TypeScript errors or bundler issues.

title: fix(editor): preserve shoe materials during decal bake
body:
## Summary
- Add reusable backend mesh cleanup for scan/import assets.
- Lock editor base material defaults to white with max roughness.
- Preserve imported shoe material slots/textures during Blender decal bake.
- Prevent baked decal meshes from being overwritten by frontend material overrides.
- Document the Blender material-slot pitfall in `AGENTS.md`.

## Root Cause
The decal bake script previously cleared/replaced base shoe mesh materials to enforce `baseColor`. Imported GLB/OBJ shoes can depend on existing material slots, texture maps, and polygon material indices, so clearing them made saved draft previews lose the original shoe appearance.

## Validation
- `./.venv/Scripts/python -m pytest` -> 26 passed
- `./.venv/Scripts/python -m ruff check app/services/decal_baker.py tests/test_decal_baker.py` -> passed
- `npm run build` -> passed, existing Vite chunk-size warning only
- `git diff --check` -> no whitespace errors, Windows CRLF warnings only

## Notes
Blender is not available in local PATH, so a real GLB bake smoke test could not be run locally. Existing previews baked before this fix should be regenerated by saving the draft again.

title: Title: fix(decal): align preview bake with shoe surface transforms
body:
## Summary
- Fix decal/text preview bake failing after Save Draft by converting GLTF transforms to Blender coordinates.
- Add surface-apply flow so selected layers store position, rotation, normal, target mesh, and offset before bake.
- Improve backend decal projection with directional raycast, outward normal handling, bounded depth, and safer offset.

## Security
- No secrets, tokens, credentials, or API keys added.
- Sticker inputs remain restricted to validated data URIs.
- Text/color/font values are sanitized before Blender bake.

## Tests
- `.\.venv\Scripts\python -m pytest`
- `.\.venv\Scripts\python -m ruff check app\services\decal_baker.py tests\test_decal_baker.py`
- `npm run build`

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## New Features

* Added **design preview generation** that bakes stickers and text decals into 3D shoe models before export.
* Introduced **sticker gallery** with categorized presets (Popular, Symbols, Nature, Sport) for quick layer creation.
* Enhanced **layer editor** with active layer selection, visibility controls, and per-layer properties panel.
* Added **surface snapping** to automatically align stickers/text to shoe geometry using 3D raycasting.
* Implemented **gizmo controls** (translate, rotate, scale) for precise layer positioning in the 3D viewer.

## Documentation

* Added comprehensive **agent skills framework** covering debugging, TDD, architecture review, and issue triage workflows.

## Tests

* Added coverage for decal baking, sticker encoding, and text layer processing.

## Chores

* Added development Docker configuration and environment setup.
